rulings.Case Title: ABRAHIM KARADSHE VS. SHERIN SAYED MOHAMED ABDELWAHAB ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Set Aside Default And Dismiss Case, And Memo Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2025, Line 7, DEFENDANT SHERIN ABDELWAHAB's Motion To Set Aside Default And Dismiss Case. Continued to January 22, 2025, to allow both sides the opportunity to provide courtesy copies per SF Local Rule 2.7B. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. MICHAEL LIPSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 583.360 For Failure To Bring Case To Trial
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2025, Line 1, DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIPSON's Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 583.360 For Failure To Bring Case To Trial. Defendant Michael Lipson's Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 583.360 For Failure To Bring Case To Trial is GRANTED. The record demonstrates that Plaintiff Bank of America, N.A. failed to bring this case, which was filed February 9, 2018, to trial within the time prescribed by Article 1 of Chapter 1.5 of the Code of Civil Procedure, even accounting for the tolling period provided in California Rules of Court, app. Emergency Rule 9. Plaintiff argued in its "Objection" that Defendant failed to serve the present motion to counsel of record's email address (nk-efile@nelson-kennard.com), as required by SF Local Rule 2.11(L). The court continued the hearing on the dismissal motion by 30 days, giving Plaintiff time to file a substantive opposition. None has been received. Defendant having made a prima facie showing that the case should be dismissed and Plaintiff having made no showing, the court orders the matter dismissed pursuant to section 583.360. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

Defendant Michael Lipson's motion to set aside default and dismiss the action pursuant to CCP 583.360 is denied without prejudice for non-compliance with CCP 1005. rulings.Case Title: LAURA WOODWARD VS. LEGACY PROBATE INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2025, Line 2. DEFENDANT LEGACY PROBATE INTERNATIONAL, INC.'s Motion To Compel Production Of Documents. Defendant Legacy Probate International's motion to compel further production of documents is GRANTED. The Brown declaration demonstrates good faith meet and confer attempts. The cited discovery appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.010.) Defendant's tenth affirmative defense contends that Plaintiff Laura Woodward's contracts are voidable and Plaintiff is entitled to quantum meruit, making the time she spent on the matters relevant. The court further notes that Plaintiff alleges an alternative cause of action for common count, which could be construed as a quantum meruit claim. (See Haggerty v. Warner (1953) 115 Cal.App.2d 468, 475 ["The count states plaintiff performed certain services for defendants, alleges their reasonable value, that they were rendered at the special instance and request of defendants, and are unpaid. This meets the requirements of a common count cast in the form of quantum meruit."]; Oliver v. Cambell (1954) 43 Cal.2d 298, 305 [referring to pleading "by common count in quantum meruit"]; Iverson, Yoakum, Papiano & Hatch v. Berwald (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 990, 996 ["where services have been rendered under a contract which is unenforceable because not in writing, an action generally will lie upon a common count for quantum meruit."].) Plaintiff's relevance objections fail for the reasons set out above. Plaintiff's overbroad and unduly burdensome objections also fail. Plaintiff's declaration does not establish the merits of those objections. Plaintiff shall provide objection-free substantive responses by January 29, 2025. Plaintiff did not act with substantial justification. Plaintiff shall also pay $2,100 in sanctions by January 29, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ).

rulings.Case Title: LVNV FUNDING LLC VS. SIOBHAN O'BOYLE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 8, 2025, Line 8, DEFENDANT SIOBHAN O'BOYLE'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. Specially-appearing Siobhan O'Boyle's motion to quash service, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. O'Boyle carried her burden and Plaintiff LVNV Funding LLC has failed to show valid service of the complaint and summons. Thus, at this time, the court is without jurisdiction over O'Boyle for purposes of this action. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
